name: GetResponse API
what_it_does: Provides REST API access to GetResponse's email marketing and automation platform for managing contacts, campaigns, autoresponders, landing pages, webinars, and transactional email sending.
use_cases: Add or update subscriber contacts with custom field values when a user completes a sign-up form or checkout flow
Trigger autoresponder sequences programmatically when a contact meets a business-defined condition (e.g., trial expiry)
Pull campaign performance statistics (opens, clicks, bounces) into a reporting dashboard on a scheduled basis
Create and send one-time newsletters via API when a new blog post or product update is published
Remove or unsubscribe contacts from lists on GDPR deletion requests routed through a privacy management agent
not_for: High-volume transactional email at scale — GetResponse is optimized for marketing campaigns; use SendGrid or Mailgun for transactional email at millions of sends per day
Enterprise CRM with complex deal pipeline management — GetResponse has basic CRM features but is not a substitute for Salesforce or HubSpot
SMS or multi-channel messaging automation beyond email and webinars
best_when: An agent needs to manage subscriber lists, trigger marketing automation sequences, and pull campaign metrics for a small-to-mid-size marketing operation without needing enterprise CRM complexity.
avoid_when: The primary need is high-volume transactional email, advanced customer journey orchestration across multiple channels, or deep CRM pipeline management.

auth.notes: API key authentication passes the key in the `X-Auth-Token` header. OAuth 2.0 is available for third-party integrations with authorization code flow. For server-to-server agent automation, API key is simpler. GetResponse MAX (enterprise) uses a separate OAuth-based API with slightly different endpoints.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan supports up to 500 contacts and 2,500 emails/month; API access is available on all plans including free.
pricing.paid_tiers: Email Marketing from $15.58/mo (1000 contacts); Marketing Automation from $48.38/mo; E-commerce Marketing from $97.58/mo; GetResponse MAX (enterprise) from $999/mo.
pricing.notes: API rate limits increase with plan tier. Free plan has lower API rate limits. MAX plan includes priority support and higher sending limits.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; typically 400 requests per hour for standard plans. Contact import operations are rate-limited separately and may be queued asynchronously.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses include a numeric code and human-readable message. Common errors (invalid email, duplicate contact) are well-described. Rate limit errors return 429 with no Retry-After header.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET and DELETE are idempotent. POST to create contacts is not — duplicate email addresses return an error rather than silently updating. Use UPSERT patterns via the contact update endpoint.
known_agent_gotchas: Contact import is asynchronous for bulk operations — the API returns immediately and agents must poll an import status endpoint to confirm completion
Campaign IDs and list IDs are opaque strings that differ between environments; agents must look them up dynamically rather than hardcoding
GetResponse MAX uses different API base URLs and slightly different request/response shapes than the standard API — integration code is not fully portable
Webhook payloads do not include HMAC signatures by default; agents consuming webhooks cannot verify authenticity without additional IP allowlisting
Unsubscribe events triggered by recipients in the email client are not immediately reflected via API — there is a processing delay before a contact's status updates

security_score_components.security_notes: API key in header is acceptable for server-side use. OAuth 2.0 available for user-delegated scenarios. No IP allowlisting documented. API keys are account-wide with no granular scoping.
